skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Verification discipline for completion claims. Use before claiming tests pass, fixes are complete, or work is ready for commit or review.
skill.summary: Require fresh verification evidence before reporting coding work as complete.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed all 14 static findings as false positives in Markdown instructions and fenced examples. No executable scripts, weak cryptography, reconnaissance behavior, network access, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fence Interpreted as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a Markdown fence or example as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The location is documentation text and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is inside SKILL.md prose or fenced Markdown examples. No script, hook, or executable command path exists at this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the line contains workflow documentation. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line describes verification behavior, not encryption or hashing. No crypto library, algorithm name, or security-sensitive operation appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, but the line contains workflow documentation. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line describes verification behavior, not encryption or hashing. No crypto library, algorithm name, or security-sensitive operation appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance, but the line only advises independent verification of an agent success report. It does not request host or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a table entry about checking results instead of trusting a report. There are no system discovery commands or data collection instructions.

content.value_statement: Unverified completion claims create false confidence in coding work. This skill requires fresh command output before reporting tests, builds, fixes, or PR readiness.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh verification commands before success claims.
Maps common claims to required evidence types.
Separates test, lint, build, bug fix, and requirement verification.
Flags weak status language such as should, probably, and seems.
Promotes independent review of delegated agent results.
limitations: Does not run tests or commands by itself.
Does not define project-specific verification commands.
Does not replace human review of complex requirements.
Works best when the repository has reliable test and build scripts.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Verification
use_cases.description: Confirm that changed code has current test, lint, or build evidence before committing work.

use_cases.title: Agent Output Review
use_cases.description: Check delegated coding work against diffs, command output, and stated requirements before accepting results.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Report clear evidence for tests, regressions, and requirement coverage before opening or updating a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Claim
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner verification before saying work is done.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before I say this task is complete, identify the verification command I should run and the evidence I need to report.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Verification Scope
prompt_templates.scenario: Select a command for a changed file or feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these changed files and my intended claim, choose the narrowest reliable verification command and explain what it proves.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Completion Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Review command output before a final response.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this verification output and tell me exactly what completion claims are supported, unsupported, or still uncertain.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Delegated Work
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of work completed by another agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the agent report, repository diff, and verification output. Identify gaps before I accept the work or open a pull request.

output_examples.input: I fixed the bug and want to say all tests pass.
output_examples.output: Run the relevant test command first. Report the command, exit status, pass count, and any remaining failures before claiming success.

output_examples.input: The linter passed, so the build should be fine.
output_examples.output: The linter result only supports a lint claim. Run the build command before saying the build succeeds.

output_examples.input: An agent says it completed the task.
output_examples.output: Check the repository diff, verify changed files, run the matching command, and report the actual state with evidence.

best_practices: Run verification in the current turn before making completion claims.
Match each claim to evidence that directly proves it.
Report failures and uncertainty plainly when verification does not pass.
anti_patterns: Claiming tests pass from memory or a previous run.
Using lint success as proof that a build or test suite passes.
Trusting an agent completion report without checking the diff and output.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It tells the agent to choose and run the right verification command before making a completion claim.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before saying a fix is complete, tests pass, a build succeeds, or a pull request is ready.

faq.question: Does lint passing prove the work is done?
faq.answer: No. Lint only proves lint status. Tests, builds, and requirements need their own evidence.

faq.question: Can it verify delegated agent work?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs the agent to inspect diffs and run independent checks before trusting another agent report.

faq.question: What evidence should be reported?
faq.answer: Report the command, exit status, important output summary, and the exact claim that the evidence supports.

faq.question: Is this skill project-specific?
faq.answer: No. It provides a general verification protocol. The project still needs its own test, lint, and build commands.
